ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Ali Nazik Lamb. Mapped to 'lamb dish' (FOODON_03311737) as the closest defensible ancestor class. Ali Nazik is a composite dish of grilled lamb mince/chunks served over smoked eggplant-yogurt puree.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat dishes — lamb-based composite dish (grilled lamb with eggplant-yogurt base); mapped under FoodEx2 composite meat dish category as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Ali Nazik. A00GJ (meat dishes) used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Composite dish includes lamb, eggplant, yogurt, garlic, butter/olive oil.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to Ali Nazik Lamb as a specific food entity. SNOMED CT not applicable for this composite dish record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant plate of Ali Nazik Lamb weighs approximately 450 g, comprising grilled lamb portions served over a generous bed of smoked eggplant-yogurt puree, typically finished with spiced butter or tomato sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation from: Lamb, ground, cooked, broiled (FDC ID 174351); Eggplant, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (FDC ID 169229); Yogurt, plain, whole milk (FDC ID 171284); combined in estimated proportions (55% lamb, 30% eggplant-yogurt base, 15% oil/butter/sauce)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Ali Nazik Lamb exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods component entries: lamb ground cooked broiled (FDC 174351), eggplant cooked boiled drained (FDC 169229), and yogurt plain whole milk (FDC 171284), combined in proportions consistent with standard Ali Nazik recipe composition (approximately 55% lamb, 30% eggplant-yogurt base, 15% oil/butter/sauce). The input record values (provenance: ai_generated, data_source: grabfood) were cross-checked against this USDA composite estimate and found broadly consistent; USDA-derived values are adopted as the authoritative basis. Sodium of 211 mg/100g is plausible given seasoning and is retained with USDA composite support. GrabFood is a delivery platform and is not used as a primary nutrient evidence source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous component GI values: yogurt plain whole milk GI 36 (Sydney GI Database entry); eggplant cooked GI ~15 (Sydney GI Database); lamb/meat GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate). Composite weighted estimate yields GI ~35.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ali Nazik Lamb as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on weighted component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, peer-reviewed): lamb/meat GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate contribution), eggplant cooked GI ~15, plain whole-milk yogurt GI ~36. Dish composition approximately 55% lamb (GI 0), 30% eggplant-yogurt base (blended GI ~25), 15% oil/butter/sauce (GI ~0). Weighted composite GI estimate: ~35. Glycemic load per 450g serving: GL = (35 × 25.2g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 8.8; conservative estimate of GL=11 used to account for sauce carbohydrate contribution.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 211 mg/100g (950 mg per 450g serving) approaches the 1000 mg per-meal threshold; potassium from lamb, eggplant, and yogurt (~290 mg/100g) partially offsets sodium load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt seasoning and limit or omit tomato paste-based sauce, keeping total sodium intake from this dish below 800 mg per serving; the eggplant-yogurt base and olive oil are beneficial for endothelial function and should be retained.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (5.6 g/100g; 25 g per serving) and low estimated GI (~35) result in a low glycemic load (~11); protein (10 g/100g) and fat slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ali Nazik Lamb is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as rice or bread; the eggplant and yogurt base provides fibre and low-GI dairy protein that support glycaemic stability, and portion size should be monitored if served with pide or lavash.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100g (18 g per 450g serving) and cholesterol at 33 mg/100g from lamb are the primary cardiovascular concern; olive oil and yogurt contribute beneficial unsaturated fats and fermented dairy respectively.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 200–250 g to keep saturated fat intake below 8 g per meal, request preparation with olive oil rather than butter-based spiced sauce, and pair with non-starchy vegetables rather than refined carbohydrate accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is a red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokine production — a hard disqualifier for RA dietary suitability; nightshade ingredients (eggplant, tomato paste, bell pepper) may additionally trigger symptom flares in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or substitute the lamb component with chicken or oily fish (e.g., salmon) to eliminate the arachidonic acid load while retaining the anti-inflammatory benefits of the eggplant-yogurt base and olive oil; nightshade sensitivity should be individually assessed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ali Nazik Lamb (also known as Ali Nazik Kebabı or Alinazik) is a classic Turkish dish originating from the Gaziantep region of southeastern Turkey. The dish consists of grilled or pan-seared lamb — typically minced or cubed — served over a bed of smoky, roasted eggplant puree blended with plain yogurt, garlic, and olive oil. The eggplant is traditionally charred directly over an open flame or under a grill to impart a distinctive smoky flavour before being peeled, mashed, and combined with strained yogurt. The lamb is seasoned with cumin, black pepper, red pepper flakes, and salt, then grilled or sautéed. The assembled dish is often finished with a drizzle of spiced butter (tereyağı) infused with red pepper flakes, or a light tomato-based sauce. The result is a harmonious contrast of textures: tender, savoury lamb against the cool, creamy, smoky eggplant-yogurt base. Nutritionally, Ali Nazik Lamb provides approximately 151 kcal per 100g, with 10g protein, 10g fat (4g saturated), and only 5.6g carbohydrate, making it a low-carbohydrate, moderate-protein dish. The low estimated glycemic index (~35) and low glycemic load (~11 per 450g serving) make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium at 211 mg/100g warrants monitoring for hypertension. Saturated fat and cholesterol from lamb represent a moderate cardiovascular concern. The dish is naturally gluten-free and halal. It is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the arachidonic acid content of red meat. A standard restaurant serving is approximately 450g, providing approximately 680 kcal.

The input record values (provenance: ai_generated, data_source: grabfood) are broadly consistent with the USDA composite estimate and have been cross-validated; USDA basis is adopted as authoritative. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values per Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is therefore medium.
